data.description:
## Confirm the verify-identity CI gate mounts the tickets store

`.github/workflows/verify-identity.yaml` runs `rebar verify-identity` (read-only merge
gate) and hard-forces `--require-authenticated` regardless of config. Investigation flagged
that the workflow may only `actions/checkout` the CODE repo and not mount the `tickets`
orphan branch (`.tickets-tracker/`), in which case `verify-identity` exits 2 ("store not
found") rather than actually scanning events.

- Verify whether the workflow mounts the tickets store; if not, add the checkout/fetch of the `tickets` branch so the gate scans real events.
- Confirm current gate state (green/red/required), given enforcement is already hard-forced in CI.
- Confirm `ENFORCE_SINCE` grandfathering is driven by the GitHub Actions repo variable `vars.ENFORCE_SINCE` (not `identity.enforce_since` in config) and document that.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `verify-identity.yaml` demonstrably scans the tickets store (does not exit 2 store-not-found).
- [ ] Current gate state documented; if the gate scans historical unsigned events, the `ENFORCE_SINCE` repo variable is set to an appropriate boundary so the gate is not spuriously red.
- [ ] The `vars.ENFORCE_SINCE` (CI) vs `identity.enforce_since` (local) distinction is documented.
data.title: Confirm verify-identity CI gate mounts the tickets store
